title: The Storm
subtitle: The dark is just the light taking a breath.

dialogue.text: The lights go out all at once. The house disappears.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: DREW!

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: I'm here. I'm right here. Nobody move — I've got the flashlight.
A click. A beam of white cuts through the dark. Drew's face appears, lit from below. He makes a monster face.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: Aaaah! ...oh. That's just you.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Just me. Scariest thing in this house.

dialogue.speaker: lu
dialogue.text: The power could be out for hours. The whole street's dark.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Good.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: Good?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: No screens. No homework. No rules. The dark just gave us permission to do whatever we want.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Flashlight tag. One flashlight. If the beam hits you, you're it. Go.
Chaos. Beautiful chaos. Running through dark hallways. Yen squealing. Lu commando-crawling behind the couch. Rush hiding behind a door.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: I hear breathing... someone's behind the curtain...

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: *giggling from behind the curtain*

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Hmm. Must've been the wind.
He walks right past Yen. On purpose. She thinks she's a genius.
Thunder shakes the house. Everyone freezes.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: ...Okay. Shadow puppet time.
Drew props the flashlight against a stack of books. The living room wall becomes a canvas.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Okay. Watch. This is a dog.

dialogue.speaker: lu
dialogue.text: That is not a dog.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: It's an artistic dog.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: Make a cat! Make a cat!

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: One cat, coming up.
His hands form something on the wall. It looks nothing like a cat. Everyone laughs anyway.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Okay, okay. Forget the puppets. I've got something better. A story.
They gather in a circle on the floor. Flashlight in the center. Faces lit gold.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Once, there were four lanterns.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: What color?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Gold, blue, rose, and yellow. Each one burned differently. The gold lantern burned the brightest — so bright it could light a whole room. But it burned the fastest.

dialogue.speaker: lu
dialogue.text: ...That's sad.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: It's not sad yet. Listen. The blue lantern burned steady. Not the brightest, but it never flickered. The rose lantern was clever — it could change its flame to match what was needed. And the yellow lantern... the yellow lantern was so small that nobody thought it mattered.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: I bet the yellow one matters.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Shh. I'm getting there. One night, a great wind came. The kind that blows out everything. And the gold lantern — the brightest one — it went out first.
Silence. Even the thunder seems to pause.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: ...Then what?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: The other three lanterns huddled together. And they found something out: when they put their flames close — not bright, just close — they made a light the wind couldn't blow out. Not because it was strong. Because it was together.

dialogue.speaker: yen
dialogue.text: What about the gold lantern?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: The gold lantern was gone. But its light wasn't. Because the others remembered how bright it burned. And every time they shone, a little bit of gold was in there too.
Nobody says anything for a long moment.

dialogue.speaker: lu
dialogue.text: Drew... is the gold lantern you?

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: It's just a story, Lu.
But the way he says it. The way he doesn't quite look at her.
The storm rumbles outside. The flashlight flickers.

choices.text: Rush puts his arm around Yen.

dialogue.text: Rush moves closer to Drew. Drew puts his arm around him without saying anything. The kind of gesture that doesn't need words.
Rush puts his arm around Yen. She leans into him. Drew watches. And for just a moment, he smiles — the kind of smile that says: you're learning.
Rush sits next to Lu. She doesn't say anything, but she leans her shoulder against his. Drew notices. Nods, just barely.

dialogue.speaker: lu
dialogue.text: I call the couch for sleeping.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: What? No way. The couch is mine. I was here first.

dialogue.speaker: lu
dialogue.text: You were NOT here first. You were in the kitchen eating crackers.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: I was scouting! That counts!

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Hey.
Just that. Just 'hey.' Both of them stop.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Yen gets the couch. She's smallest. You two get the floor cushions. I'll take the rug.

dialogue.speaker: rush
dialogue.text: The rug is the worst spot.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: Good thing I'm the toughest, then.
He always takes the worst spot. Every time. Rush is just now noticing.
Later. The storm has softened to rain. Yen is asleep on the couch, one arm hanging off. Lu is curled on the floor cushions, breathing slow.
Drew is on the rug. He's not asleep. His eyes are open, staring at the ceiling.
Lightning flashes. For a moment, two green eyes are visible at the window. Watching.
Drew turns his head. Sees the eyes. Doesn't flinch.

dialogue.speaker: drew
dialogue.text: ...I know you're there.
The eyes blink. Slowly. Then they're gone.
Drew watches the window for a long time. Then he closes his eyes.
The rain falls. Four kids sleep. One of them is awake just long enough to make sure the others are safe.
